title: fix: stop community model updates from silently reactivating disabled endpoints
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- `POST /:id/update` unconditionally cleared `disabledAt`/`disabledReason`/`disabledBy` on every save, even edits unrelated to the broken config
- Confirmed in prod: MarcosFRG's deactivated models kept reactivating themselves (traced to their dashboard session resaving, clearing the flag as a side effect, not a deliberate reactivate action)
- Reactivation is now exclusively manual/out-of-band — owner pings to reactivate after fixing the issue; a dedicated reactivate flow is a follow-up

## Test plan
- [x] `enter.pollinations.ai` targeted tests pass (community-endpoint-openai, admin-auth)
- [x] typecheck + biome clean

gist.headline: No more zombie endpoints
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky bug where updating a disabled community model would accidentally wake it back up. Now, deactivated endpoints stay peacefully asleep until manually revived.
gist.summary: Updating a community model via POST /:id/update no longer automatically clears the disabledAt, disabledReason, and disabledBy flags.
gist.impact: Deactivated community models will no longer silently reactivate when their owners edit unrelated configuration settings.
gist.visual_concept: A sleeping server or robot representing a deactivated endpoint, with Polly the bee carefully working around it without waking it up.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny nightcap, tiptoeing past a sleeping chunky server robot that is tucked in with a cozy blanket. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm dim lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, peaceful vibes.
